insights.title: Highest Profit Margin
insights.detail: NVIDIA Corporation leads with a 55.8% profit margin, converting $130.5B revenue into $72.8B profit.

insights.title: Lowest Profit Margin
insights.detail: Amazon.com Inc. has the lowest margin at 5.3%, with $575.0B revenue and $30.4B profit.

insights.title: Top 5 by Market Cap
insights.detail: These five companies represent the largest valuations in the S&P 500, dominating the tech and finance sectors.

insights.title: Top 5 by TTM Revenue
insights.detail: Revenue leaders show the scale of operations across retail, tech, and energy sectors.

insights.title: Top 5 by Net Profit
insights.detail: These companies are the most profitable, generating the highest net income over the trailing twelve months.

insights.title: Best 12-Month Performer
insights.detail: Meta Platforms Inc. stock gained 68.2% over the past 12 months, the best performance among the top 20.

insights.title: Worst 12-Month Performer
insights.detail: UnitedHealth Group Inc. stock declined 42.5% over the past 12 months, reflecting sector or company-specific challenges.

insights.title: Average Metrics (Top 20)
insights.detail: The average top-20 S&P 500 company has a market cap of $1T+ and generates over $200B in annual revenue.

insights.title: Revenue vs Market Cap Outliers

high.detail: Higher ratio suggests undervaluation relative to revenue

low.detail: Lower ratio suggests premium valuation or lower revenue efficiency

insights.detail: Revenue-to-market-cap ratio highlights potential valuation anomalies. A higher ratio may indicate undervaluation.

insights.title: Total Market Value of Top 20
insights.detail: The combined market capitalization of the top 20 S&P 500 companies is $25.16 trillion, representing a significant portion of the entire index's value.
